question_text: A student burns 2.0 grams of peanuts in a calorimeter. The energy released heats 100 cm³ of water from 20°C to 45°C. Calculate the energy content of the peanuts in joules per gram. (Specific heat capacity of water = 4200 J/kg°C)
correct_answer: Energy released = mass × specific heat capacity × temperature change = 0.1 kg × 4200 J/kg°C × 25°C = 10,500 J. Energy per gram = 10,500 J ÷ 2.0 g = 5,250 J/g or 5.25 kJ/g
explanation: This multi-step problem requires calculating the total energy transferred to water using Q = mcΔT, then dividing by the mass of peanuts burned to find energy per gram. This is how calorimeters determine food energy content.
